channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rubio kicks off Mideast trip in Israel as Arab leaders reel from Trump's Gaza proposal
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is kicking of a Mideast tour in Israel on Sunday, as Arab leaders reel from President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population of the Gaza Strip to other countries and redevelop it under U.S. ownership.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US presented Ukraine with a document to access its minerals but offered almost nothing in return
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he directed his ministers not to sign off on a proposed agreement to give the United States access to Ukraine’s rare earth minerals because the document was too focused on U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Emmanuel Macron calls 'emergency meeting' for European leaders to discuss Trump: report
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has allegedly planned an "emergency meeting" to discuss President Donald Trump on Monday, according to a Polish official.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump officials to begin peace talks with Russia in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, National Security Adviser Mike Waltz and Special Envoy to the Middle East Steve Witkoff are likely to meet with senior Russian officials in Saudi Arabia in the coming days to discuss an end to Russia's war in Ukraine, NewsNation reported on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: A 23-year-old man stabbed 6 people in Austria killing 1 in what police describe as a random attack
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — A 23-year-old man stabbed six passersby in southern Austria on Saturday in what police said was a random attack that left a 14-year-old dead and five others injured. The suspect was detained in the city of Villach, where the attack took place, police said.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LICHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels advance and Congolese forces retreat from eastern Congo's second largest city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Panic swept through eastern Congo’s second-largest city on Saturday as residents and soldiers fled by the thousands, scrambling to escape the looming advance of Rwanda-backed rebels.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, SAM METZ and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel releases over 300 Palestinian prisoners after receiving 3 more hostages
item.description: Israel and Hamas completed their sixth exchange of hostages and prisoners on Saturday after their ceasefire survived its latest crisis. The Palestinian militant group was going to delay the exchange because Israel wasn’t letting the agreed-upon humanitarian aid into the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American detained in Russia identified as Kalob Wayne Byers
item.description: Kalob Wayne Byers was identified as the American citizen recently detained at an airport in Moscow, Russia, on a drug smuggling charge, media reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 23-year-old man stabbed 6 people in Austria killing 1 in what police describe as a random attack
item.description: A 23-year-old man has stabbed six passersby in southern Austria in what police said was a random attack that left a 14-year-old dead and five others injured.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump foreign aid freeze deals heavy blow to family planning efforts abroad
item.description: The Trump administration’s 90-day freeze on foreign aid has impacted family planning resources used by hundreds of thousands of women, girls and couples abroad, causing a number of health clinics to shut down and hindering the supply chain for contraception.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, The Hill

item.title: A 2-year-old girl and her mother have died after the car-ramming attack in Munich
item.description: Police say a 2-year-old girl and her mother have died two days after they were injured in the car-ramming attack on a labor union demonstration in Munich.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump lauds latest hostage release, says US will support Israel's decision on ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Saturday celebrated the latest hostage release, when Hamas freed the last living Israeli American from captivity in Gaza, but warned the Palestinian militant group that the U.S. will support Israel's decision on whether the fragile ceasefire continues.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings, attempted murders
item.description: Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings and attempted murders against Israelis are among the 369 released Saturday by Israel in exchange for hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Crunch time for hostages amid ceasefire uncertainty
item.description: Fox News Correspondent Greg Palkot spoke with the daughter and sister of two released hostages. Both noted the positive role played by President Trump in helping to free their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis will remain in hospital, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis will remain in the hospital for treatment, the Vatican said. He was admitted on Friday after holding audiences due to respiratory issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: Some white South Africans have showed support for President Donald Trump and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: Alfonso Nqunjana And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance meets with leader of far-right German party
item.description: Vice President Vance met Friday with a leader of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party, making him the highest-ranking U.S. official to do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels advance into eastern Congo's 2nd major city of Bukavu, residents say
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo entered the region’s second-largest city of Bukavu on Friday, local and civil society leaders said, the latest ground gained since a major escalation of their yearslong fighting with government forces.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nurses suspended after viral video threatening to kill Israeli man: Australia Health Department
item.description: Two nurses in Australia were suspended following a viral video showed them threatening to kill Israeli patients.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican president signals possible lawsuit against Google over Gulf of America name change
item.description: Mexico could sue Google over its change of the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America on its Google Maps interface in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he and Putin agreed to begin ‘negotiations’ on ending Ukraine war
item.description: Trump says he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin "negotiations" on ending the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. How to lower your risk
item.description: Polluted air, laden noxious gasses or tiny, invisible particles that burrow into human bodies, kills 7 million people prematurely every year, the U.N. health agency estimates.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump says ceasefire deal should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all hostages by Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that a precarious ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all the remaining hostages it is holding in Gaza by midday on Saturday — though he also said that such a decision would be up to Israel.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and SAM MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
